plugin.name: PortalValue
plugin.author: Eric
plugin.purpose: Find the closest saved portal to a newly-entered chaos portal and show the speedwalk.

muclient.script:
local MAPPER_ID = "b6eae87ccedd84f510b74714" local GMCP_ID = "3e7dedbe37e44942dd46d264"
-- theme colours local C = { hdr = "@x099", -- purple val = "@x045", -- cyan num = "@W", -- bright white ok = "@x048", -- green bad = "@R", -- bright red (diagnostics) }
-- state pv_history = pv_history or {} pv_debug = pv_debug or false
-- colour output (fall back to plain text if the colour lib isn't available) local have_colors = pcall(require, "aardwolf_colors") function pv_echo(s) if have_colors then AnsiNote(ColoursToANSI(s)) else -- crude strip of @-codes Note((s:gsub("@x%d%d?%d?", ""):gsub("@%a", ""))) end end
-- escape user-supplied text so stray @ signs aren't read as colour codes local function esc(s) return (tostring(s or ""):gsub("@", "@@")) end
-- sql string-quote local function q(s) return "'" .. tostring(s):gsub("'", "''") .. "'" end
local CARDINALS = { n=true, s=true, e=true, w=true, u=true, d=true }
-- turn an ordered list of step commands into a compressed speedwalk + a move count local function build_speedwalk(dirs) local segs, run = {}, {} local moves = 0 local function flush() if #run > 0 then local parts = {} for _, rc in ipairs(run) do parts[#parts+1] = ((rc.c > 1) and rc.c or "") .. rc.d end segs[#segs+1] = "run " .. table.concat(parts) run = {} end end for _, d in ipairs(dirs) do moves = moves + 1 if CARDINALS[d] then local last = run[#run] if last and last.d == d then last.c = last.c + 1 else run[#run+1] = { d = d, c = 1 } end else flush() segs[#segs+1] = d -- custom exit, verbatim end end flush() return table.concat(segs, ";"), moves end
-- format the "no-portal walking distance from recall" line local function recall_line(rooms, sw, msg) if rooms ~= nil then if rooms == 0 then return C.hdr .. "Recall walk : " .. C.val .. "you're standing on recall" end return C.hdr .. "Recall walk : " .. C.num .. rooms .. C.hdr .. " rooms (no portals) " .. C.ok .. (sw ~= "" and esc(sw) or "") end return C.hdr .. "Recall walk : " .. C.hdr .. "(" .. esc(msg or "unknown") .. ")" end
-- locate the mapper's database file on disk. -- Everything here is read from the running client at runtime, so it adapts to -- whatever world / character / install the plugin is loaded into: -- * the mapper's own "db_name" variable (the file it actually uses) -- * GetInfo(66) = this world's file folder (where the mapper keeps the db) -- Returns: path (or) nil, reason local function get_db_path() if not IsPluginInstalled(MAPPER_ID) then return nil, "the Aardwolf GMCP mapper plugin is not installed" end local db_name = GetPluginVariable(MAPPER_ID, "db_name") if not db_name or db_name == "" then -- fallback: replicate the mapper's own default = sanitize_filename(WorldName()) db_name = (WorldName() or ""):gsub("[^%w%s()_-]", "") db_name = db_name:gsub("^%s+", ""):gsub("%s+$", "") -- Trim end if db_name == "" then return nil, "could not determine the mapper's database name" end local path = GetInfo(66) .. db_name .. ".db" local f = io.open(path, "rb") if not f then return nil, "database file not found at: " .. path end f:close() return path end
-- pretty-print one result record local function pv_show_record(r, with_header) if with_header then pv_echo(C.hdr .. "=================== " .. C.num .. "PortalValue" .. C.hdr .. " ===================") end pv_echo(C.hdr .. "This room : " .. C.val .. esc(r.newname) .. " " .. C.num .. "[" .. esc(r.newuid) .. "] " .. C.hdr .. "(" .. C.val .. esc(r.area) .. C.hdr .. ")") local areanote = "" if r.same_area == false then areanote = C.hdr .. " (in " .. C.val .. esc(r.parea or "?") .. C.hdr .. ")" end pv_echo(C.hdr .. "From portal : " .. C.val .. esc(r.pname) .. " " .. C.num .. "[" .. esc(r.puid) .. "]" .. areanote) pv_echo(C.hdr .. " command : " .. C.ok .. esc(r.pcmd) .. " " .. C.hdr .. "(lvl " .. C.num .. esc(r.plevel) .. C.hdr .. ")") pv_echo(C.hdr .. "Speedwalk : " .. C.ok .. (r.sw ~= "" and esc(r.sw) or "(adjacent)")) pv_echo(C.hdr .. "Movements : " .. C.num .. r.moves .. C.hdr .. " walk + " .. C.num .. "1" .. C.hdr .. " portal = " .. C.num .. (r.moves + 1)) pv_echo(recall_line(r.recall_rooms, r.recall_sw, r.recall_msg)) end
local function save_history() SetVariable("history", serialize.save_simple(pv_history)) end
-- core: given a room uid, find the shortest-walk portal across ALL areas and the -- no-portal walking distance from recall, then report. -- If "channel" is given (e.g. "spo", "clan", "tell bob"), send a one-line summary -- to that channel instead of printing the full local report. local function compute_for_room(T, channel) T = tostring(T)
local dbpath, why = get_db_path() if not dbpath then pv_echo(C.hdr .. "[PortalValue] " .. esc(why or "could not locate the mapper database") .. ". Type " .. C.ok .. "pvcheck" .. C.hdr .. " for details.") return end
local ok_sql, sqlite3 = pcall(require, "sqlite3") if not ok_sql then pv_echo(C.hdr .. "[PortalValue] sqlite3 library unavailable.") return end
local db = sqlite3.open(dbpath) if not db then pv_echo(C.hdr .. "[PortalValue] Could not open the mapper database.") return end
-- landing room area + name local area, tname for row in db:nrows(string.format("SELECT area, name FROM rooms WHERE uid=%s", q(T))) do area = row.area tname = row.name end if not area then db:close() pv_echo(C.hdr .. "[PortalValue] Landing room " .. C.num .. esc(T) .. C.hdr .. " isn't mapped yet, so I can't compute a path.") return end
-- if you already have a portal straight to this room, no speedwalk is needed local known = false for _ in db:nrows(string.format( "SELECT 1 AS x FROM exits WHERE fromuid IN ('*','**') AND touid=%s LIMIT 1", q(T))) do known = true end if known then db:close() pv_echo(C.hdr .. "[PortalValue] " .. C.val .. "You already have a portal to this room, why do you need another? Greed is destroying the world.") return end
-- load ALL walking exits (incl. cexits) and build a reverse adjacency, so we -- can breadth-first search the whole map backward from this room local radj = {} for row in db:nrows("SELECT fromuid AS f, touid AS t, dir AS d FROM exits WHERE fromuid NOT IN ('*','**')") do local t = tostring(row.t) local bucket = radj[t] if not bucket then bucket = {}; radj[t] = bucket end bucket[#bucket+1] = { from = tostring(row.f), dir = row.d } end
-- every saved portal (any area), tagged with its area so we can prefer local ones local portals = {} for row in db:nrows([[ SELECT e.touid AS t, e.dir AS d, e.level AS lv, r.name AS nm, r.area AS ar FROM exits e JOIN rooms r ON r.uid = e.touid WHERE e.fromuid IN ('*','**') ]]) do portals[#portals+1] = { uid = tostring(row.t), cmd = row.d, level = row.lv, name = row.nm, area = row.ar, } end
-- recall room = destination of your recall/home portal (prefer one named "recall") local recall_uid for row in db:nrows("SELECT touid AS t, dir AS d FROM exits WHERE fromuid='**'") do if not recall_uid then recall_uid = tostring(row.t) end if row.d and tostring(row.d):lower():find("recall") then recall_uid = tostring(row.t) break end end db:close()
-- global backward BFS from this room local dist = { [T] = 0 } local fwd = {} -- fwd[node] = { dir =, nxt = } forward step toward T local queue, head = { T }, 1 while head <= #queue do local cur = queue[head]; head = head + 1 local d0 = dist[cur] local preds = radj[cur] if preds then for _, pe in ipairs(preds) do local p = pe.from if dist[p] == nil then dist[p] = d0 + 1 fwd[p] = { dir = pe.dir, nxt = cur } queue[#queue+1] = p end end end end
-- reconstruct the forward dir list from some start room to T local function path_dirs(start) local dirs, node = {}, start while node ~= T do local step = fwd[node] if not step then return nil end dirs[#dirs+1] = step.dir node = step.nxt end return dirs end
-- no-portal walking distance from recall local recall_rooms, recall_sw, recall_msg if not recall_uid then recall_msg = "no 'recall' portal saved in the mapper" elseif recall_uid == T then recall_rooms, recall_sw = 0, "" elseif dist[recall_uid] == nil then recall_msg = "no known no-portal walk from recall yet" else recall_sw, recall_rooms = build_speedwalk(path_dirs(recall_uid)) end
-- shortest reachable portal by walking distance, across ALL areas (a portal in -- another area can give a shorter total path than an in-area one). Ties break -- toward a portal in this same area. local best for _, pt in ipairs(portals) do if pt.uid ~= T and dist[pt.uid] ~= nil then local d = dist[pt.uid] if (not best) or d < best.d or (d == best.d and pt.area == area and best.portal.area ~= area) then best = { d = d, portal = pt } end end end
if not best then pv_echo(C.hdr .. "[PortalValue] No saved portal has a known walking path to " .. C.val .. esc(tname or "?") .. " " .. C.num .. "[" .. esc(T) .. "]" .. C.hdr .. " yet. (Explore the connecting rooms, or save a nearby portal.)") pv_echo(recall_line(recall_rooms, recall_sw, recall_msg)) return end
local sw, walkmoves = build_speedwalk(path_dirs(best.portal.uid))
local rec = { time = os.date("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M"), area = area, newname = tname or "?", newuid = T, pname = best.portal.name or "?", puid = best.portal.uid, parea = best.portal.area or "?", same_area = (best.portal.area == area), pcmd = best.portal.cmd or "?", plevel = tostring(best.portal.level or 0), sw = sw, moves = walkmoves, recall_rooms = recall_rooms, recall_sw = recall_sw, recall_msg = recall_msg, } table.insert(pv_history, rec) save_history()
if channel then -- one-line channel summary (no speedwalks). Recall shows N/A when there is -- no known no-portal walking route from recall to here. local recall_disp = (recall_rooms ~= nil) and tostring(recall_rooms) or "N/A" local summary = C.hdr .. "Room " .. C.num .. T .. C.hdr .. ": [" .. C.num .. (walkmoves + 1) .. C.hdr .. " movements from " .. C.num .. best.portal.uid .. C.hdr .. "] [" .. C.num .. recall_disp .. C.hdr .. " portaless movements from recall" .. C.hdr .. "]" Send(channel .. " " .. summary) pv_echo(C.hdr .. "[PortalValue] Sent summary to " .. C.val .. esc(channel) .. C.hdr .. ".") return end
pv_show_record(rec, true) end
-- read the current room's vnum from GMCP, then run the calculation. -- channel (optional) routes a one-line summary to that channel instead. local function process_current_room(channel) local _, data = CallPlugin(GMCP_ID, "gmcpval", "room.info") local room pcall(function() room = assert(loadstring("return " .. (data or "nil")))() end) if type(room) ~= "table" or not room.num then pv_echo(C.hdr .. "[PortalValue] Couldn't read the current room from GMCP (try LOOK first).") return end compute_for_room(tostring(room.num), channel) end
-- ===== alias entry points (must be global) =====
function pv_test() process_current_room() end
-- "pv <channel>" -> post a one-line summary to that channel function pv_channel(name, line, wc) local channel = (wc[1] or ""):gsub("^%s+", ""):gsub("%s+$", "") if channel == "" then process_current_room() else process_current_room(channel) end end
function pv_history() if #pv_history == 0 then pv_echo(C.hdr .. "[PortalValue] History is empty.") return end pv_echo(C.hdr .. "========= " .. C.num .. "PortalValue history (" .. #pv_history .. ")" .. C.hdr .. " =========") for i, r in ipairs(pv_history) do local pnote = (r.same_area == false) and (C.hdr .. " (in " .. C.val .. esc(r.parea or "?") .. C.hdr .. ")") or "" local rnote = r.recall_rooms and (C.hdr .. " recall " .. C.num .. r.recall_rooms .. C.hdr .. "r") or "" pv_echo(C.hdr .. string.format("%2d. ", i) .. C.val .. esc(r.newname) .. " " .. C.num .. "[" .. esc(r.newuid) .. "] " .. C.hdr .. "<- " .. C.val .. esc(r.pname) .. " " .. C.num .. "[" .. esc(r.puid) .. "]" .. pnote) pv_echo(" " .. C.ok .. (r.sw ~= "" and esc(r.sw) or "(adjacent)") .. C.hdr .. " (" .. C.num .. r.moves .. "+1" .. C.hdr .. " moves, " .. C.val .. esc(r.area) .. C.hdr .. ", " .. esc(r.time) .. ")" .. rnote) end end
function pv_purge() pv_history = {} save_history() pv_echo(C.hdr .. "[PortalValue] History cleared.") end
function pv_toggle_debug() pv_debug = not pv_debug pv_echo(C.hdr .. "[PortalValue] Debug " .. C.num .. (pv_debug and "ON" or "OFF") .. C.hdr .. ".") end
-- self-diagnostics: confirms the plugin can see this install's mapper + database. -- Safe to run on anyone's machine; tells them exactly what's missing if anything. function pv_check() pv_echo(C.hdr .. "=================== " .. C.num .. "PortalValue check" .. C.hdr .. " ===================")
local has_mapper = IsPluginInstalled(MAPPER_ID) pv_echo(C.hdr .. "Mapper plugin : " .. (has_mapper and (C.ok .. "found") or (C.bad .. "NOT installed")))
local has_gmcp = IsPluginInstalled(GMCP_ID) pv_echo(C.hdr .. "GMCP handler : " .. (has_gmcp and (C.ok .. "found") or (C.bad .. "NOT installed")))
local path, why = get_db_path() if not path then pv_echo(C.hdr .. "Database : " .. C.bad .. esc(why or "not found")) pv_echo(C.bad .. "Not ready. Make sure the Aardwolf mapper is installed and you have") pv_echo(C.bad .. "looked at a room at least once so the mapper has built its database.") return end pv_echo(C.hdr .. "Database : " .. C.ok .. esc(path))
local ok_sql, sqlite3 = pcall(require, "sqlite3") if not ok_sql then pv_echo(C.hdr .. "SQLite : " .. C.bad .. "library unavailable") return end local db = sqlite3.open(path) if not db then pv_echo(C.hdr .. "SQLite : " .. C.bad .. "could not open the database") return end local nrooms, nportals = 0, 0 for row in db:nrows("SELECT count(*) AS c FROM rooms") do nrooms = row.c end for row in db:nrows("SELECT count(*) AS c FROM exits WHERE fromuid IN ('*','**')") do nportals = row.c end db:close() pv_echo(C.hdr .. "Rooms mapped : " .. C.num .. nrooms) pv_echo(C.hdr .. "Saved portals : " .. C.num .. nportals) if nportals == 0 then pv_echo(C.ok .. "Ready, " .. C.hdr .. "but you have no saved mapper portals yet. Add some with the") pv_echo(C.hdr .. "mapper's " .. C.val .. "'mapper portal <command>'" .. C.hdr .. " before " .. C.val .. "pv" .. C.hdr .. " can find anything.") else pv_echo(C.ok .. "Ready. Stand in a room and type " .. C.val .. "pv" .. C.ok .. ".") end end
function pv_usage() pv_echo(C.hdr .. "=================== " .. C.num .. "PortalValue" .. C.hdr .. " ===================") pv_echo(C.val .. "Stand in a room and find the nearest saved portal + speedwalk to it.") pv_echo(C.hdr .. " pv " .. C.val .. "- calculate for the room you're in") pv_echo(C.hdr .. " pv <channel> " .. C.val .. "- post a one-line summary to a channel (e.g. pv spo)") pv_echo(C.hdr .. " portalvaluehistory " .. C.val .. "- list all results") pv_echo(C.hdr .. " portalvaluepurge " .. C.val .. "- clear the history") pv_echo(C.hdr .. " pvcheck " .. C.val .. "- verify it's set up correctly on this client") end
-- ===== lifecycle =====
function OnPluginInstall() require "serialize" local saved = GetVariable("history") if saved and saved ~= "" then local ok, t = pcall(function() return assert(loadstring("return " .. saved))() end) if ok and type(t) == "table" then pv_history = t else pv_history = {} end else pv_history = {} end pv_echo(C.hdr .. "[PortalValue] Loaded. " .. C.num .. #pv_history .. C.hdr .. " saved result(s). Type " .. C.ok .. "portalvalue help" .. C.hdr .. " for help.") -- Verify setup automatically so a new user knows immediately whether it works. -- Deferred a couple seconds so it isn't fooled by plugin load order at startup -- (the mapper may not have finished loading at our install moment). DoAfterSpecial(2, "pv_check()", 12) -- 12 = sendto.script end
function OnPluginSaveState() save_history() end
